name: 33_Object_Detection_YOLOv3_COCO_DL
query: Help me develop an object detection system using the YOLOv3 model and the COCO dataset. Download the dataset and preprocess the images by resizing and normalization in `src/data_loader.py`. Implement the YOLOv3 model and use Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) to refine the results in `src/model.py`. Save the detected objects to `results/figures/`, and create an interactive Streamlit web page in `src/app.py` to display the detection results. Finally, evaluate the model's performance, including metrics such as mAP and inference time, and save the evaluation results to `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`. The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application to prevent unnecessary resource usage.

requirements.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.
"Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.
Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.
An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.
Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

preferences.criteria: The "Streamlit" web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily upload and view new images for detection.
The performence evalution includes mAP and inference time as metrics.
The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application.

judge_stats.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/data_loader.py` does not implement COCO dataset downloading. The file only contains placeholder logic: `load_dataset()` returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and there is no download code, no network/file retrieval, no COCO API usage, and no path/extraction handling. The mention of `"COCO"` appears only in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`/`KEYWORDS`, not in executable download functionality.

judge_stats.criteria: Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement actual preprocessing for resizing or normalization. The only relevant function, `build_transforms()`, merely returns a placeholder list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` and contains no image-processing code, imports like `torchvision.transforms`, or logic applying resize/normalize operations to images.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement a YOLOv3 model. The file only contains metadata strings like `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA = ["The \"YOLOv3\" model is implemented in \`src/model.py\`.", ...]` and a placeholder function `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. There is no YOLOv3 architecture, layers, forward pass, or model-loading logic present.

judge_stats.criteria: "Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/model.py` does not implement Non-Maximum Suppression logic. The file only defines metadata like `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`, `requirement_notes()`, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. There is no NMS function, no box-overlap filtering logic, and no detection refinement code in the provided snippet.

judge_stats.criteria: Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> No evidence shows detection outputs being written to `results/figures/`. The workspace contains the folder, but the provided code in `src/data_loader.py` and `src/model.py` only includes placeholder loader/model functions and no file-saving logic (e.g., no image export, plotting, or write operations). The trajectory evidence also explicitly states there are no run logs or file-operation entries verifying saves to `results/figures/`.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/app.py` does not implement an interactive Streamlit web page. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a `main()` function returning a dictionary. There is no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI code, and no logic to read or display detection results from `results/figures/`. Additionally, no execution evidence shows a Streamlit app was created or run.

judge_stats.criteria: Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/model_performance.txt` exists, its contents are only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is a placeholder rather than actual saved model performance evaluation results. No trajectory evidence shows any write/save action producing real evaluation metrics.
